sources.notes: Read the move-to-front-or-back transition and bounds: n <= 1e9, sum q <= 200000.
The official implementation represents all reachable positions using three intervals and updates their endpoints in constant time per operation.

Cross-checked the exact transition for a reachable position y relative to the selected position x. The article uses more elaborate set maintenance, so it was used primarily to verify the local transition.

Derived an interval-union transition and compared it with explicit reachable-position sets on 10000 randomized tests. Every result matched, and the reachable set always contained at most three disjoint intervals.

reference_candidates.notes: Additional Luogu solution index; direct access was not required for this review.

annotation.summary: After each nondeterministic move of the card at position ai to either end of the deck, report how many different positions the joker could occupy.
annotation.constraints: n can be as large as 1e9 and total q is at most 200000, so reachable positions cannot be represented individually.
annotation.core_idea: For a reachable joker position y and operation x: if y<x, the next position is y or y+1; if y>x, it is y-1 or y; and if y=x, moving the joker itself sends it to 1 or n. Starting from {m}, these transitions preserve the invariant that the reachable set is the union of at most three intervals: a prefix touching 1, at most one middle interval, and a suffix touching n. Update each interval according to its relation with x, create the two boundary positions when x is reachable, merge overlaps, and sum the interval lengths.
annotation.complexity: O(q) time and O(1) additional memory per test case.
tricks: Write the exact transition according to whether the joker lies before, after, or at the moved card.
Compress up to n reachable positions into at most three intervals.
Allow prefix, middle, and suffix intervals to overlap and merge them when counting.
When x is reachable, replace that branch by the two boundary positions 1 and n.

solution_variants.name: three-reachable-intervals
solution_variants.summary: Maintain a prefix interval, an optional middle interval, and a suffix interval representing every reachable joker position; update and merge them in constant time after each operation.
solution_variants.complexity: O(q) time and O(1) additional memory.

tags.evidence: The boolean reachability function over positions 1..n is stored through its at most three true interval runs, removing all dependence on the enormous n.
Correctness and constant complexity depend on proving that every transition preserves a union of at most three intervals.
Each interval update follows the exhaustive cases x left of the interval, x right of it, or x contained in it.
The final code requires careful interval merging and empty-interval handling, but these details are not the conceptual retrieval target.
